name: Northern Cardinal
description: Bright red songbird commonly found in backyards and forests. Known for its loud, clear whistling song.

name: Blue Jay
description: Bold blue bird with a crest and loud calls. Often seen in forests and suburban areas.

name: American Goldfinch
description: Small yellow bird that feeds on seeds. Common in fields and backyard feeders.

name: Red-tailed Hawk
description: Large hawk often seen soaring over open areas. Recognized by its reddish tail.

name: Mourning Dove
description: Soft gray bird with a long tail and gentle cooing call. Frequently seen on the ground or perched on wires.

name: Downy Woodpecker
description: Small black and white woodpecker with a short bill. Common in forests and backyard trees.

name: Barn Swallow
description: Fast-flying bird with a forked tail. Often seen swooping over fields and near buildings.

name: Ruby-throated Hummingbird
description: Tiny bird that hovers while feeding on nectar. Males have a bright red throat.

name: Carolina Chickadee
description: Small bird with a black cap and white cheeks. Known for its cheerful call.

name: Tufted Titmouse
description: Gray bird with a pointed crest and large eyes. Common at feeders and wooded areas.

name: American Crow
description: Large black bird known for its intelligence. Often seen in groups and heard giving loud caws.

name: American Robin
description: Common bird with a red-orange breast. Often seen hopping across lawns searching for worms.

name: Bald Eagle
description: Large bird of prey with a white head and tail. Found near lakes and rivers.

name: Baltimore Oriole
description: Bright orange and black bird that builds hanging nests. Often found in trees during summer.

name: Black-capped Chickadee
description: Small bird with a black cap and friendly behavior. Common in wooded areas and feeders.

name: Carolina Wren
description: Small brown bird with a loud, musical song. Often found in shrubs and brushy areas.

name: Cedar Waxwing
description: Sleek bird with a crest and smooth brown plumage. Often seen in flocks feeding on berries.

name: Chimney Swift
description: Fast, darting bird that spends most of its time in flight. Nests inside chimneys and hollow structures.

name: Common Grackle
description: Glossy black bird with iridescent feathers. Often found in large flocks in open areas.

name: Eastern Bluebird
description: Bright blue bird with a rusty chest. Often seen perched on fences or in open fields.

name: Purple Finch
description: Rosy-colored finch that appears irregularly in winter. More common in some years than others.

name: Red-breasted Nuthatch
description: Small bird that climbs down tree trunks headfirst. Appears irregularly in fall and winter.

name: Snowy Owl
description: Large white owl rarely seen in Missouri. Appears during winter irruption years.

name: Sandhill Crane
description: Tall gray bird with a red crown and loud call. Seen during spring and fall migration.

name: Dark-eyed Junco
description: Small gray bird often seen hopping on the ground. Common in Missouri during winter months.

name: White-throated Sparrow
description: Brown sparrow with a white throat and yellow spots near the eyes. Frequently seen in winter at feeders and brushy areas.

name: Brown Creeper
description: Small brown bird that spirals up tree trunks searching for insects. Blends in well with bark.

name: Winter Wren
description: Tiny brown bird with a short tail and loud song. Found in dense vegetation during winter.

name: Yellow-rumped Warbler
description: Gray warbler with yellow patches on its sides and rump. One of the few warblers seen in winter.

name: Wood Duck
description: Colorful duck with a distinctive crested head. Found in wooded wetlands and ponds.

name: Northern Shoveler
description: Duck with a large spoon-shaped bill. Seen in Missouri mainly during migration.

name: Blue-winged Teal
description: Small duck with a pale blue wing patch. Common during spring and fall migration.

name: Least Sandpiper
description: Small shorebird with brown streaking. Often seen along muddy shorelines during migration.

name: Spotted Sandpiper
description: Shorebird with spots on its belly and a bobbing walk. Seen near water in summer and migration.

name: Whooping Crane
description: Large white crane with black wing tips. Extremely rare visitor during migration.

name: Mississippi Kite
description: Graceful gray raptor that feeds on insects in flight. Found in Missouri during summer.

name: Broad-winged Hawk
description: Small hawk known for migrating in large groups. Seen mainly during spring and fall migration.

name: Short-eared Owl
description: Medium-sized owl often seen flying over open fields at dusk. More common in winter.

name: Northern Saw-whet Owl
description: Very small owl with a round face and bright eyes. Rare and mostly seen in winter.

name: Common Yellowthroat
description: Small yellow warbler with a black mask. Found in dense vegetation near water.

name: Yellow Warbler
description: Bright yellow bird with a sweet song. Common near streams and wetlands.

name: Black-throated Green Warbler
description: Green-backed warbler with a black throat. Seen mainly during migration.

name: Magnolia Warbler
description: Yellow warbler with bold black streaks. Common during migration.

name: Blackpoll Warbler
description: Small streaked warbler that migrates long distances. Seen mainly in fall migration.

name: Tennessee Warbler
description: Plain greenish warbler with a sharp call. Common during migration.

name: Nashville Warbler
description: Small warbler with a gray head and yellow body. Seen during migration.

name: Chestnut-sided Warbler
description: Yellow-capped warbler with chestnut sides. Found in brushy areas during summer.

name: Wilson’s Warbler
description: Bright yellow warbler with a black cap. Seen during migration.

name: Cape May Warbler
description: Colorful warbler with streaked chest and yellow face. Seen mainly during migration.

name: Chipping Sparrow
description: Small sparrow with a rusty cap and clear song. Common in summer.

name: Clay-colored Sparrow
description: Pale sparrow with a simple face pattern. Seen mainly during migration.

name: Henslow’s Sparrow
description: Secretive sparrow found in tall grass. Rare and localized in Missouri.

name: Grasshopper Sparrow
description: Small sparrow with a buzzy song. Found in grasslands during summer.

name: Vesper Sparrow
description: Brown sparrow with white outer tail feathers. Seen in fields during migration.

name: Lincoln’s Sparrow
description: Finely streaked sparrow with a buffy chest. Seen mainly during migration.

name: White-crowned Sparrow
description: Boldly striped sparrow with black and white head. Common in winter.

name: Lark Sparrow
description: Striking sparrow with a patterned face. Found in open areas during summer.

name: LeConte’s Sparrow
description: Small orange-faced sparrow. Rare migrant in Missouri.

name: Harris’s Sparrow
description: Large sparrow with a black face. Seen during migration and winter.

name: Canvasback
description: Large diving duck with a sloping forehead. Seen during migration.

name: Redhead
description: Diving duck with a reddish head. Found in lakes during migration.

name: Ring-necked Duck
description: Dark duck with a subtle neck ring. Common during migration.

name: Lesser Scaup
description: Diving duck with a blue bill. Seen in large flocks during migration.

name: Bufflehead
description: Small diving duck with a large white head patch. Seen in winter.

name: Hooded Merganser
description: Small duck with a fan-shaped crest. Found in wooded wetlands.

name: Common Merganser
description: Large fish-eating duck with a narrow bill. Seen in winter.

name: Ruddy Duck
description: Small stiff-tailed duck. Males have bright blue bills.

name: Trumpeter Swan
description: Large white swan with a loud call. Seen in Missouri during winter.

name: Tundra Swan
description: White swan similar to the Trumpeter. Seen during migration.

name: Golden Eagle
description: Large dark eagle rarely seen in Missouri. Occurs mainly in winter.

name: Swainson’s Hawk
description: Long-winged hawk seen in open areas. Rare migrant in Missouri.

name: Ferruginous Hawk
description: Large pale hawk of open country. Rare visitor to Missouri.

name: Black Vulture
description: Dark vulture with short tail. Increasing in Missouri.

name: Northern Goshawk
description: Powerful forest hawk. Rare and irregular in Missouri.

name: Long-eared Owl
description: Medium owl with long ear tufts. Secretive and seen mainly in winter.

name: Burrowing Owl
description: Small ground-dwelling owl. Rare visitor to Missouri.

name: Great Gray Owl
description: Large northern owl. Extremely rare in Missouri.

name: Barn Owl
description: Pale owl with a heart-shaped face. Rare but present in Missouri.

name: Elf Owl
description: Tiny owl rarely recorded in Missouri. Considered an accidental visitor.

name: Eastern Towhee
description: Black and orange bird that scratches in leaf litter. Common in brushy areas.

name: Bobolink
description: Black and white bird with a bubbly song. Found in grasslands during summer.

name: Orchard Oriole
description: Smaller oriole with chestnut coloring. Seen in summer.

name: Yellow-headed Blackbird
description: Blackbird with a bright yellow head. Seen in wetlands during migration.

name: Rusty Blackbird
description: Dark bird with rusty edges in winter. Seen near wetlands.

name: Common Loon
description: Diving bird with a haunting call. Seen on lakes during migration.

name: Horned Grebe
description: Small diving bird with red eyes. Seen during migration and winter.

name: Pied-billed Grebe
description: Small brown diving bird with a thick bill. Found in wetlands.

name: American Coot
description: Dark waterbird with a white bill. Common in ponds and lakes.

name: Sora
description: Small rail with a short yellow bill. Found in marshes during migration.
